title: 10 best herbal capsules for phlegm

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with phlegm because they contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This compound helps to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat and lungs, reducing excess mucus production and alleviating congestion. Additionally, licorice root has been traditionally used to expel phlegm and mucus from the body, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving coughs, colds, and sinusitis. As a result, herbal licorice capsules can provide fast and effective relief from phlegm-related respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce swelling and congestion in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel excess mucus.
Liquiritin: This flavonoid has antiviral and antimicrobial properties, which helps to combat infections that can cause phlegm buildup in the throat and lungs.
Licoric Acid: This compound has expectorant properties, which helps to loosen and clear out excess mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps with phlegm by its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making it easier to expel mucus from the respiratory tract.
Saponins: Assists in reducing phlegm by their anti-inflammatory and demulcent properties, which help soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and congestion.
Flavonoids: Contributes to reducing phlegm by their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate inflammation and promote the breakdown of mucus, making it easier to expel.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to thin and loosen phlegm, making it easier to expel.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to relieve phlegm and cough.
Seeds: They are also a good source of glycyrrhizin and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote the expulsion of phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots, stored in an airtight container for 2 years or less.
Grind 50g of the harvested roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 20g of the powdered root with 10g of gelatin in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Add 50ml of distilled water to the mixture, heat until gelatin dissolves, then cool for 30 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the cooled mixture, 500mg per capsule, and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with phlegm because they contain Echinacea, a natural herb that has been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues. The herb's anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties help to reduce mucus production in the body, making it easier to breathe and clear out excess phlegm. Additionally, Kansas coneflower capsules have been shown to strengthen the immune system, which can help to fight off underlying infections that may be contributing to excessive phlegm. By taking these capsules, individuals may experience relief from congestion and coughing.
remedy_properties: Echinacein: Echinacein has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel excess mucus and phlegm.
Cynarin: Cynarin is a saponin that helps break down and clear out excess phlegm from the lungs and airways, relieving congestion and coughing.

Iridoid glycosides: Iridoid glycosides have expectorant properties, which help loosen and facilitate the removal of mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, providing relief from coughs and congestion.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds may help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and promote the clearance of mucus, thereby alleviating symptoms associated with phlegm.

Alkylphenols: As a type of phenolic compound, alkylphenols may exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which could help reduce the presence of pathogens that contribute to phlegm production.
Triterpene Saponins: These compounds may possess expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up phlegm.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make capsules for phlegm due to their high content of echinacoside, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules for phlegm as they contain compounds like echinacin, which may help reduce inflammation and combat respiratory issues.

Rhyzomes: They are used to make capsules for phlegm due to their rich content of alkaloids, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may help alleviate respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 pounds of echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall after the first frost.
Dry the roots for 2-3 weeks in a warm well-ventilated area to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the ground roots with 2 parts of a filler material such as starch or cellulose in a ratio to create 1000 capsules.
Fill 1000 empty capsules with the echinacea angustifolia root powder using a capsule filling machine or a spoon for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with phlegm because they contain mucilage, a soothing and protective substance that coats the throat and respiratory tract. This natural barrier helps to trap excess mucus, allowing it to be easily cleared from the body without causing discomfort or coughing fits. The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow root also help to reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion. As a result, herbal marshmallow capsules provide gentle and effective relief from phlegm-related symptoms.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow root contains a thick, gel-like substance called mucilage, which helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel phlegm.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow extract has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs, making it easier to breathe and clear out excess mucus.
Expectorant effects: The saponins present in marshmallow root have been found to stimulate the production of saliva and mucus, helping to loosen and expel phlegm from the respiratory tract.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and providing a physical barrier against irritants that can exacerbate phlegm production.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, possess anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the airways, making it easier to expel phlegm.
Triterpene saponins: Triterpene saponins, such as althaea saponin, have been shown to have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear phlegm from the airways, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the most commonly used part due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.
Leaves: They are used due to their similar mucilage content to the roots, making them effective in reducing phlegm and relieving coughs.
Stems: They are sometimes used as a substitute for roots or leaves, although their mucilage content may be lower, still providing some relief for phlegm and coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh roots of althaea officinalis when they are at their peak maturity and cut them into small pieces.
Dry the root pieces in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours to preserve their potency.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill gelatin capsules with 0.2 to 0.5 grams of the ground root powder and seal them tightly.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with phlegm because they contain natural compounds that have been traditionally used to soothe and expel excess mucus from the respiratory tract. The capsules are rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and iridoid glycosides, which work together to reduce inflammation and congestion, making it easier to breathe and clear out phlegm from the lungs and airways. Regular consumption of elder capsules can also help to thin and break up mucus, allowing for a more effective cough and making it easier to expel phlegm from the body.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Elder capsules can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up and expel phlegm.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to congestion and excess phlegm production.
Antiviral properties: The antiviral compounds in elder capsules may help combat viral infections such as the common cold or flu, which can cause excessive mucus production and phlegm buildup.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds help reduce inflammation and congestion, making it easier to expel phlegm from the respiratory system.
Salicin: A type of glycoside, salicin has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate congestion and make it easier to cough up phlegm.
Volatile oils (specifically, borneol): These oils have expectorant properties, helping to thin and loosen phlegm, making it easier to expel from the respiratory system.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in bioactive compounds, Sambucus nigra flowers are primarily used in herbal remedies to help alleviate respiratory issues, including phlegm.
Leaves: Sambucus nigra leaves are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce congestion and excess phlegm.
Fruits (or Berries): Sambucus nigra berries are commonly used in supplements to help combat respiratory infections and reduce phlegm due to their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves on a dry sunny morning.
Dry the harvested material in a single layer at 25-30 degrees celsius for 24-48 hours.
Grind 5-10 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 1-2 teaspoons of gelatin and 1-2 tablespoons of distilled water to create a gel-like mixture.
Fill a capsule with 250-500 milligrams of the sambucus nigra gel mixture and seal the capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with phlegm because of its natural ability to soothe and clear the respiratory tract. The capsule's active compounds, such as fumaric acid and potassium, help to break down and expel excess mucus and phlegm, relieving congestion and coughs. Additionally, fennel's expectorant properties aid in thinning and loosening phlegm, making it easier to cough up and clear from the lungs and airways. This natural remedy provides quick relief from respiratory issues and promotes overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Fennel's expectorant property helps to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up and breathe easier.
Antitussive property: Fennel's antitussive property helps to suppress coughing spasms, reducing the frequency and intensity of coughing fits.

Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to chronic respiratory conditions like bronchitis and asthma.
remedy_constituents: Foeniculin: Foeniculin, a sesquiterpene lactone, helps reduce phlegm by inhibiting mucus production and secretion in the respiratory tract.
Bergapten: Bergapten, a furanocoumarin, exhibits expectorant properties, which helps to break down and clear phlegm from the respiratory system.
Anethole: Anethole, a phenylpropene, possesses antispasmodic and expectorant properties, helping to relax airway muscles and facilitate the removal of phlegm.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are primarily used due to their high concentration of volatile oils, particularly anethole, which is responsible for their medicinal properties and ability to relieve phlegm.
Leaves: The leaves of Foeniculum vulgare are utilized for their carminative and expectorant properties, making them effective in relieving phlegm and coughs.
Stems: The stems of the plant are also used, particularly for their fiber content, which can aid in digestion and relieve phlegm-related issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in the morning when the plant is dry and free of moisture.
Dry the foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in a warm place for 7 to 10 days.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 250 milligrams of the foeniculum vulgare powder with 100 milligrams of a carrier like tapioca starch.
Fill gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with phlegm because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated respiratory passages. The menthol content in peppermint oil helps to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel from the body. Additionally, peppermint's expectorant qualities help to loosen and clear out excess phlegm, providing quick relief from congestion and coughing fits. remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are primarily used to make capsules for phlegm because they contain high concentrations of essential oils that help in reducing mucus and relieving respiratory issues.

Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are sometimes used to make capsules for phlegm due to their ability to carry the plant's medicinal properties, including its expectorant and decongestant qualities.

Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita are occasionally used in combination with other parts to make capsules for phlegm, as they are believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties that aid in soothing respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of mentha x piperita leaves when the plant is in full bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 30-40°c for 2-3 days to remove moisture.
Grind 20g of dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powdered leaves with 50g of vegetable cellulose powder in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill 50 empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the herbal mixture each for storage and use.

This natural remedy can provide relief from persistent coughs, congestion, and sinus pressure associated with conditions such as bronchitis, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), and asthma, promoting clear airways and improved lung function.

Phenolic acids (specifically ferulic acid and caffeic acid): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate the underlying causes of phlegm production and reduce its viscosity.

Flavonoids (specifically isorhapontigenin): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to break down and clear out phlegm, making it easier to expel and relieving respiratory congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots: The roots of Boswellia serrata are commonly used to make capsules for phlegm due to their high concentration of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Boswellia serrata are often used to make capsules for phlegm as they contain boswellic acids, which help in reducing inflammation and congestion.
Barks: Barks: The barks of Boswellia serrata are used to make capsules for phlegm because they contain boswellic acids and terpenes, which have decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of dried boswellia serrata resin powder using a digital scale.
Mix the resin powder with 500 grams of cornstarch in a large bowl.
Add 1 teaspoon of silica as an anti-caking agent and mix well for 2 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the boswellia serrata mixture using a capsule filler.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with phlegm because they contain bioactive compounds that have natural expectorant properties, effectively breaking down and loosening stubborn mucus in the lungs and airways. The bitter flavonoids and taraxasterol present in dandelion also help to stimulate the production of saliva and digestive enzymes, reducing inflammation and congestion in the respiratory tract. By promoting healthy mucus clearance, herbal dandelion capsules can provide relief from persistent coughs, congestion, and sinus pressure associated with excessive phlegm production.
remedy_properties: Laxative Properties: Dandelion capsules stimulate digestion and relieve constipation, which can help to reduce the accumulation of mucus and phlegm in the body.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Dandelion contains flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms such as congestion and coughing.

Mucilages and Fiber: Dandelion capsules contain mucilages (soft, gel-like substances) and fiber that can help to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing mucus production and making it easier to expel phlegm.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: A triterpenoid saponin that helps in reducing inflammation and mucus production in the body, thereby alleviating phlegm-related respiratory issues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh taraxacum officinale roots and dry them completely in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Grind 500 grams of dried taraxacum officinale roots into a fine powder using a high-speed blender or coffee grinder.
Sift the powder through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles and obtain 400 grams of smooth powder.
Mix 400 grams of taraxacum officinale powder with 100 grams of gelatin and 50 grams of glycerin in a clean glass bowl.
Fill 100-200 vegetarian capsules with the gelatin mixture using a capsule filling machine or a small spoon for manual filling.

Clean the root to remove any debris or contamination using a fine mesh sieve and water.
Dry the cleaned root at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours using a low-temperature oven.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder with a 40-mesh screen.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder at a dosage of 400 milligrams per capsule.

Leaves: They are used to make capsules for phlegm, with their purported ability to help reduce inflammation and clear mucus and phlegm from the body.

Grind the dried materials into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.

Leaves: Containing rosmarinic acid, a natural expectorant that helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, providing relief from congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves measure 100g of leaves.
Dry the harvested leaves using a warm air circulation dryer for 24 hours.
Grind dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of ground melissa officinalis with 250mg of natural beeswax and 100mg of vitamin e oil.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared mixture and seal tightly for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with phlegm because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes. The capsules' active ingredients, particularly flavonoids and carotenoids, work to thin out excess mucus, making it easier to expel from the lungs. Additionally, pot marigold's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out phlegm congestion, allowing for more efficient breathing and improved respiratory health. This natural remedy provides a gentle yet effective solution for relieving phlegm-related discomforts.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot Marigold's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and congestion in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel excess mucus and alleviate coughs.
Expectorant: The plant's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear out thick phlegm from the lungs and airways, providing relief from stubborn coughs and congestion.

Antioxidant: Pot Marigold's antioxidant properties can help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by excess mucus production, reducing inflammation and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain a high concentration of expectorant properties, making them effective in loosening and removing excess mucus from the respiratory tract.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are used to create medicinal capsules that help to break down and clear out phlegm buildup in the lungs, promoting easier breathing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula officinalis flowers at full bloom in the morning.
Dry the flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 8 hours or in the sun for 3 days.
Grind 20 grams of dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered calendula with 80 grams of empty gelatin capsules in a bowl.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed capsules helps with phlegm because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the respiratory tract. The herb's active compounds, including taraxasterol and beta-amyrin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and open up airways, making it easier for the body to expel phlegm and other respiratory secretions. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate congestion and coughs associated with colds, flu, and allergies.
remedy_properties: Eupatory properties: Joe Pye Weed capsules can help to expel excess mucus from the body by stimulating the expulsion of phlegm from the respiratory system, promoting easy coughing up of congestion.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects: The bioactive compounds in Joe Pye Weed capsules have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing phlegm production.
Expectorant properties: Joe Pye Weed capsules contain expectorants that help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and bronchial passages, allowing for easier breathing and relief from congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in expectorant properties, the roots help loosen and remove excess mucus from the lungs and airways.
Rhyzomes: As a natural demulcent, rhyzomes soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eupatorium perfoliatum leaves and flowers in mid-summer for optimal potency and efficacy.
Dry the collected eupatorium perfoliatum herb material using low heat for 24 hours or air-drying for several weeks to preserve its bioactive compounds.
Grind the dried eupatorium perfoliatum herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder, taking care not to over-process and retain essential oils.
Mix 1 part of the powdered eupatorium perfoliatum herb with 2 parts of organic cornstarch or silica dioxide as filler and capsules fillers to create a consistent blend.
Fill gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the prepared herbal blend, using approximately 250-500mg per capsule, and seal them tightly to preserve potency and freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein capsules helps with phlegm because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The herb's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and silicic acid, work together to reduce swelling in the airways and sinuses, making it easier to expel excess mucus and phlegm. This natural relief can help alleviate symptoms of chronic bronchitis, asthma, and allergies, promoting clear breathing and a healthy respiratory system.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Mullein contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, helping to reduce inflammation and congestion caused by excess phlegm.
Saponins: The saponins in mullein have expectorant properties, meaning they help to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up and expel.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce swelling and oxidative stress in the respiratory tract, further alleviating symptoms of phlegm buildup.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the mullein plant are used to make capsules as they contain a high concentration of mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm irritated respiratory tract tissues.

Buds: Mullein buds are used in capsule production due to their rich content of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and ease congestion caused by phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Dry harvested verbascum thapsus leaves in a warm environment for several days.
Grind dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Sift powdered verbascum thapsus through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles 10-15 grams of powdered herb per capsule fill.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with sifted powdered verbascum thapsus using a funnel or scoop.
Store filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lilac capsules helps with phlegm because they contain a potent blend of herbal extracts that work in harmony to combat excess mucus production. The capsules' primary active ingredient, lilac extract, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier drainage of phlegm. Additionally, other herbs such as thyme and eucalyptus help break down stubborn mucus, making it easier to expel. As a result, lilac capsules provide effective relief from congested sinuses and chesty coughs associated with excessive phlegm production.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal lilac capsules contain compounds like linoleic acid, which has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and congestion associated with excess phlegm production.

Expectorant: The mucilages present in herbal lilac capsules have an expectorant property, which helps to loosen and expel mucus from the lungs and respiratory tract, providing relief from coughs and congestion.

Antimicrobial: Herbal lilac capsules contain antimicrobial compounds that can help combat infections that may contribute to phlegm production, such as bronchitis or sinusitis, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in expectorant compounds, lilac roots help to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract.
Rhyzomes: Lilac rhyzomes contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the chest and lungs, allowing for easier removal of phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried flowers of syringa vulgaris, 10 grams, and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered syringa flowers with 20 grams of honey, 5 grams of glycerin, and 50 grams of starch in a clean glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring frequently, to create a smooth and consistent paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules, size 0, with 400 milligrams of the syringa flower-honey-glycerin-starch paste each, and seal them tightly.
Label and store the prepared capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for phlegm are natural supplements that help alleviate excessive mucus production, congestion, and coughing. These capsules contain a blend of herbal extracts that work together to thin and clear mucus, providing relief from respiratory issues. The best herbal capsules for phlegm include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Ephedra sinica, Zingiber officinale, Pulmonaria officinalis, Pinellia ternata, Ginkgo biloba, Piper nigrum, Echinacea purpurea, Thymus serpyllum, and Eucalyptus globulus. If left untreated, phlegm can lead to fatigue, difficulty breathing, and recurrent respiratory infections, severely impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to phlegm that are treated with herbal capsules are coughs, colds, and bronchitis. Herbal remedies such as slippery elm, marshmallow root, and licorice root help soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and congestion. Capsules containing herbs like eucalyptus, peppermint, and ginger also help ease respiratory issues by loosening mucus and opening airways, providing relief from coughs and congestion.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with phlegm is a blend of Echinacea, Ginger, and Licorice root. Echinacea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce congestion, while Ginger's natural expectorant properties aid in loosening and clearing mucus. Licorice root, with its soothing properties, helps to calm the respiratory tract and reduce inflammation. This combination helps to break down and eliminate excess phlegm, promoting a clear and healthy respiratory system. Regular use can provide relief from respiratory issues.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Frontiers in pharmacology, herbal capsules containing extracts of medicinal plants such as Siraitia grosvenorii, Lonicera japonica, Platycodon grandiflorus, and Glycyrrhiza uralensis can be beneficial for phlegm due to their ability to significantly reduce pharyngeal symptoms and signs in patients with chronic pharyngitis. The study involved 52 patients in the experimental group and 51 patients in the control group, who were given either botanical lozenges or placebos for 15 days. The results showed that the total score of pharyngeal symptoms in the experimental group was significantly lower than that in the control group, with improvement rates of 73.81% for pharyngeal itching, 67.50% for dry throat, 67.57% for pharyngeal foreign body sensation, 65.22% for aggravation due to excessive speaking, and 44% for congestion of pharyngeal mucosa.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with phlegm because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The extract helps to thin and loosen phlegm, making it easier to expel from the body. Additionally, licorice root has natural demulcent properties, which soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat and airways. This can help to reduce congestion and promote a more productive cough, ultimately providing relief from phlegm-related respiratory issues. Regular use may also improve overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with phlegm by reducing inflammation and suppressing the production of mucus in the airways, thus alleviating respiratory issues.
Liquiritin: It aids in phlegm relief by acting as an anti-inflammatory and expectorant, facilitating the removal of mucus from the lungs and airways.

Licoricidin: It helps with phlegm by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, thereby reducing the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that can contribute to respiratory infections and phlegm accumulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to reduce phlegm production.
Leaves: They contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin and other flavonoids that aid in reducing inflammation and relieving phlegm-related symptoms.
Seeds: Although less commonly used, seeds also contain glycyrrhizin and other compounds that may help to reduce phlegm production and alleviate associated symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Crush 500g of dried root of glycyrrhiza glabra into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with 20g of gelatin and 10g of glycerin in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Gradually add 10ml of distilled water to the mixture while stirring with a spoon for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a gelatin capsule machine and fill 100 capsules in 20 minutes.
Label the capsules with the name and dosage of the herbal product and store them in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica capsules helps with phlegm because of its natural expectorant properties. The plant's active compounds, such as ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, work to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel from the body. This process helps to alleviate congestion and coughs associated with respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. By promoting the clearance of phlegm, Ephedra sinica capsules provide relief for individuals experiencing respiratory discomfort, promoting a healthier and more balanced respiratory system.
remedy_constituents: Ephedrine: It helps to reduce phlegm by acting as a decongestant, thereby relieving congestion and promoting the clearance of mucus from the respiratory tract.
Norsesquiterpene alkaloids: These compounds help to reduce phlegm by exhibiting expectorant properties, which aid in the removal of excess mucus from the airways.
Ephedrine-derived alkaloids: They help to reduce phlegm by increasing the secretion of mucus-thinning substances and enhancing the clearance of mucus from the respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used to make capsules for phlegm due to their ability to dry up dampness and relieve phlegm congestion.
Seeds: Seeds are used for their warm and drying properties, which help to relieve phlegm and promote expectoration.
Leaves: Leaves are used to make capsules for phlegm due to their ability to dry up dampness, relieve phlegm congestion, and promote sweating.
remedy_recipe: Dry the ephedra sinica plant material in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried ephedra sinica plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 milligrams of the ground ephedra sinica powder with 250 milligrams of cellulose powder in a bowl.
Fill 100-count gelatin capsules with the ephedra sinica mixture using a capsule filler machine.
Store the herbal ephedra sinica capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with phlegm because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The active compound, gingerol, has been shown to thin mucus and promote its expulsion from the body, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, ginger has been traditionally used to relieve congestion and coughs associated with colds and flu. By reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract, ginger capsules can help alleviate symptoms of phlegm and promote overall respiratory health. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective alternative to pharmaceuticals.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion associated with phlegm.
Shogaols: Shogaols have expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract.

Terpenoids (specifically, bisabolol and ginger terpenes): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, which help reduce inflammation and open airways to facilitate the removal of phlegm.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used because they contain a high concentration of gingerols, which help to relieve respiratory issues and reduce mucus production.
Buds: Used because they are rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried zingiber officinale root from a reputable supplier to ensure quality and freshness.
Grind 250g of the harvested root into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 10g of the powdered root with 10g of gelatin in a bowl to create the capsule filling.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 0.25g of the root mixture each using a small spoon.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container to preserve their potency and shelf life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Pulmonaria officinalis capsules helps with phlegm because they contain the natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties of the plant. The saponins and other bioactive compounds in Pulmonaria officinalis help to break down and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion. By thinning and loosening phlegm, Pulmonaria officinalis capsules provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and chronic coughs, promoting a clearer airway and improved breathing. This natural remedy supports the body's natural healing process.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds help break down and clear phlegm from the airways by their surfactant and expectorant properties.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Pulmonaria officinalis have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, making it easier to expel phlegm.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Pulmonaria officinalis exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help alleviate respiratory infections and reduce the production of excess phlegm.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because they contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that can help soothe and relieve phlegm-related respiratory issues.
Roots: They are used because they are rich in saponins, compounds that can help thin and expel phlegm from the respiratory system.
Stems: They are used because they contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation and relieve congestion associated with phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh pulmonaria officinalis leaves and flowers at dawn for maximum potency retention.
Dry the harvested material in a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 8 hours to preserve properties.
Grind 50 grams of the dried pulmonaria officinalis material into a fine powder using a mill.
Mix 10 grams of the ground powder with 90 grams of vegetable cellulose to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the powder blend using a capsule filler device.

remedies.remedy_desc: Pinellia ternata capsules helps with phlegm because they have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to clear lung heat and reduce mucus production. The herb is believed to have a drying effect on the body, which helps to eliminate excess phlegm and promote a healthy respiratory system. By clearing the lungs and relieving congestion, Pinellia ternata capsules may help to alleviate symptoms of coughs, colds, and other respiratory conditions associated with excess phlegm. It's often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its effects.
remedy_constituents: Tetradine: It helps to break up and discharge phlegm by stimulating the respiratory system and increasing mucus secretion.
Pinellin: It has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to relieve congestion and promote the clearance of phlegm from the airways.
Palmitin: It has a drying effect on the respiratory system, which helps to reduce the production of excess mucus and alleviate symptoms of phlegm accumulation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes are used to make capsules for phlegm because they are believed to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Roots: The roots are used to make capsules for phlegm because they are thought to help reduce mucus and relieve respiratory issues.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make capsules for phlegm because they are believed to have a soothing effect on the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Dry pinellia ternata tubers in a well-ventilated area for at least 3 days at 35 degrees celsius temperature.
Crush dried pinellia ternata tubers into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 6 grams of the fine powder with 30 grams of gelatin and 20 grams of glycerin.
Fill the gelatin-glycerin mixture into empty gelatin capsules with 4-6 capsules per dose.
Label and store the herbal capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with phlegm because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba work together to thin and break down mucus, making it easier to expel. Additionally, it improves blood circulation to the lungs, which helps to clear excess phlegm and congestion. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy respiratory function, Ginkgo biloba capsules can provide relief from phlegm-related respiratory issues, such as coughs and congestion. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to a treatment plan for respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help reduce inflammation and congestion by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.
Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide): Reduce mucus production and inflammation, thereby alleviating cough and respiratory issues associated with phlegm.
Ginkgolides (specifically Ginkgolide B): Inhibit the release of histamine and other chemical mediators responsible for allergic reactions, including those that cause phlegm production and congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their high flavonoid and terpene content, which helps in reducing inflammation and promoting expectoration.
Seeds: They are often used in traditional medicine due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which aid in relieving cough and congestion.
Barks: They are also used due to their potential anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, which may help in relieving respiratory issues such as phlegm and cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees during fall season when leaves turn golden yellow.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Crush 100 grams of dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 5 grams of powdered ginkgo biloba with 5 grams of cellulose powder and 1 gram of silica gel.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filling machine within 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum capsules helps with phlegm because they contain the bioactive compound piperine, which has potent anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. This helps to break down and clear excess mucus from the respiratory system, making it easier to breathe. The piperine in Piper nigrum capsules also has an antiviral effect, which can help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to phlegm production. This makes Piper nigrum capsules a popular natural remedy for relieving respiratory congestion.
remedy_constituents: Piperine: It helps to break down and reduce the production of phlegm by its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Piperonal: It helps to thin and clear out phlegm by its decongestant and bronchodilatory effects.
Eugenol: It helps to reduce the viscosity of phlegm and alleviate respiratory congestion by its mucolytic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used due to their piperine content, which has expectorant properties to help loosen and clear phlegm.
Leaves: Used due to their volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties to ease respiratory issues.
Fruits: Used due to their piperine and flavonoid content, which has antioxidant and expectorant properties to reduce inflammation and promote phlegm clearance.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500g of piper nigrum berries in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried piper nigrum berries into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 250g of the powdered piper nigrum with 250g of gelatin and 250g of water.
Pour the mixture into a capsule machine and fill 250 capsules.
Label and store the filled capsules in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with phlegm because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to boost the immune system. The herb contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid that help reduce inflammation and congestion in the body, making it easier to breathe and expel mucus. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea has been traditionally used to treat colds and respiratory infections, which can cause excessive phlegm production. By supporting the body's natural defenses, Echinacea purpurea capsules may help alleviate phlegm-related symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and fight off infections that contribute to excessive phlegm production.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Echinacea have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate congestion and cough associated with phlegm.
Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates stimulate the immune system, which can help the body fight off underlying infections that cause excessive phlegm production.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of compounds that help in reducing inflammation and alleviating respiratory issues.
Leaves: They are utilized because they contain essential oils and compounds that aid in relieving congestion and coughs.
Flowers: They are employed as they possess anti-inflammatory properties and are used to treat respiratory problems like bronchitis and coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of echinacea purpurea roots and flowers from a clean dry area to avoid contamination.
Dry the harvested material for 2 weeks in a warm dry environment with good air circulation to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried roots and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part of the powdered echinacea with 1 part of a binding agent such as gelatin or starch to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the echinacea blend and store them in an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus serpyllum capsules helps with phlegm because they contain the natural antiseptic properties of thyme, which effectively breaks down and eliminates excess mucus and phlegm from the respiratory system. The expectorant properties of thymus serpyllum help to loosen and clear congestion, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, thymus serpyllum has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and soothe the mucous membranes, providing relief from coughs, colds, and respiratory infections. This natural remedy can promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear phlegm from the respiratory tract.
Carvacrol: Exhibits mucolytic properties, breaking down thick phlegm and making it easier to cough up.
Rosmarinic acid: Possesses anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation in the airways and alleviating congestion associated with phlegm.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory system.
Flowers: They contain essential oils that aid in relieving congestion and coughs by promoting the expulsion of phlegm.
Seeds: They possess antispasmodic properties, which help in relieving coughs and reducing the production of phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Dry the thymus serpyllum flowers and leaves to prevent mold and preserve the essential oils for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried thymus serpyllum flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 400 milligrams of the powdered thymus serpyllum with 100 milligrams of a binding agent such as gelatin for 10 minutes.
Fill a capsule shell with the thymus serpyllum mixture and seal the capsule using a manual capsule machine.
Store the herbal thymus serpyllum capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus capsules helps with phlegm because they contain the essential oils extracted from the leaves of the Eucalyptus tree, which have potent decongestant and expectorant properties. These properties help to break down and clear out thick, sticky phlegm from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and expel. By reducing inflammation and congestion, Eucalyptus globulus capsules promote a free flow of mucus, providing relief from coughs, colds, and respiratory infections, and helping to alleviate symptoms associated with phlegm buildup.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): It acts as a bronchodilator, helping to relieve congestion and reduce phlegm production by opening up airways.
α-Pinene: It has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to break down and clear out phlegm, making it easier to expel.
Limonene: It has bronchodilatory effects, which can help to relieve coughs and congestion by expanding airways and reducing phlegm production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of essential oils, particularly eucalyptol, which helps relieve respiratory issues and congestion.
Buds: They contain a high concentration of eucalyptol and other compounds that help to break down and clear phlegm from the airways.
Leaves (again): Leaves are often used in combination with buds to create a more potent formula for relieving respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning to ensure peak oil content and quality.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm place for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture.
Grind 20 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 gram of the ground powder with 3 grams of gelatin in a small bowl.
Encapsulate the mixture using a capsule machine to produce 100 herbal eucalyptus globulus capsules.
